title: Process Intelligence (Senior) Consultant (m/w/d)
matched_skills: Business Intelligence / BI (Power BI PL-300 certified)
missing_skills: Consulting experience in client-facing data/process projects
key_requirements: Strong SQL, BI, and database skills — Celonis experience strongly preferred
Minimum 1 year in data, process, or automation domain
Degree in Business Informatics, Computer Science, Mathematics or related field
Ability to work independently in client projects with professional communication
cv_highlights: Microsoft Power BI Certified (PL-300) — covers BI and dashboard requirements partially
KPI framework design and data storytelling at HSBC and Hapag-Lloyd
Cross-functional project delivery and governance across complex programmes
Fluent in German, English, Chinese, and Cantonese — language requirement fully met
Technology operations and tech stack lifecycle management background
gaps: No SQL or database skills demonstrated in CV
No Celonis or any Process Mining platform experience
No ETL or data pipeline/modeling experience
No consulting or client-facing advisory role experience
Degree field not confirmed to be in a technical/STEM discipline
recommended_emphasis: Highlight Power BI certification and any dashboard/analytics work prominently
Emphasise process analysis and KPI reporting work as adjacent to process intelligence
Stress German fluency and cross-cultural communication skills
Frame PMO governance work as process transparency and optimisation experience
Consider adding any SQL exposure even if limited (e.g. ad-hoc queries, BI tool data connections)
recommendation: Weak match. The role is highly technical, centred on Celonis Process Mining, SQL, ETL, and automation — none of which appear in Boru's CV. While Power BI certification and data storytelling experience provide partial overlap on the BI/dashboard dimension, the core platform and programming requirements are unmet. Applying would require significant upskilling messaging or is likely to be screened out at resume review. Not recommended unless Boru can credibly demonstrate SQL and process mining interest/coursework.
